script: As the disciples battled a fierce storm on the Sea of Galilee, Jesus walked toward them on the water. Terrified, they thought he was a ghost—until he spoke. Peter stepped out, walking on water too—until fear made him sink. Jesus rescued him, calming the storm. Amazed, the disciples worshipped him as the Son of God.
shots: Dark, churning waves violently rock a small wooden boat filled with panicked disciples of Jesus, clutching the sides.
A distant Jesus-like figure emerges from the storm, walking calmly on the turbulent water as lightning flashes behind him.
The disciples of Jesus huddle together on a boat, wide-eyed and trembling, pointing at the approaching figure with terrified expressions.
The apostle Peter, hesitantly stepping over a boat's edge, takes his first steps on the water, his face a mix of awe and determination.
Peter's expression shifts to fear as he begins to sink, arms flailing, while Jesus reaches out to grasp his hand.
The storm instantly stills, the waves now gentle, as the disciples kneel in the boat, gazing up at Jesus with reverence.
